relational.title: Relational
relational.strength: You trust through face-to-face bonds, personal loyalty, and repeated good faith.
relational.weakness: Relational trust does not scale beyond the people you know, and it can survive too long past a betrayal.
relational.fits: small teams, close partnerships, contexts where loyalty and judgment matter most.

contractual.title: Contractual
contractual.strength: You trust through clear written terms that define what happens when things break.
contractual.weakness: Contracts cannot cover every contingency; legalistic trust can hollow out the goodwill it relies on.
contractual.fits: partnerships with real stakes, strangers, situations where the downside must be bounded.

reputational.title: Reputational
reputational.strength: You trust through track records and references — what others can independently verify.
reputational.weakness: Reputation lags reality and concentrates on whoever is already visible, hiding newcomers and slow drift.
reputational.fits: markets, networks, and contexts where past behavior predicts future behavior.

procedural.title: Procedural
procedural.strength: You trust through transparent process, audits, and verifiable steps — trust that survives personnel changes.
procedural.weakness: Process can become performance; people learn to game the audit instead of the work it measures.
procedural.fits: regulated, high-stakes, and repeatable work where trust must outlast individuals.

communal.title: Communal
communal.strength: You trust through shared governance and collective accountability — trust distributed across a group.
communal.weakness: Communal trust can diffuse responsibility until no one is accountable; it needs real teeth.
communal.fits: cooperatives, commons, and mission-driven ventures where trust must bridge many people.

pairInsight: When your top mechanism diverges sharply from a partner's top mechanism, partnerships work when you both recognize each mechanism has a context where it is the right tool — not when you argue yours is correct.

shareTexts.invite: How do you build and verify trust?
shareTexts.minimal: My trust blueprint is {primary}. What is yours?
shareTexts.full:
My trust blueprint is {primary}, with {secondary} as a backstop.
What is yours?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders comparing how they build trust and where their mechanisms complement or clash. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
